content.text: Golf League Rules — 2026 Season
Andover Municipal Golf Course • Wednesdays • 9 Holes
Mulligans
Everyone gets 1 free mulligan to use each week. You CAN use mulligans for putts this year.
Hazards / Out of Bounds

For pace of play, if you hit into the water, out of bounds, or anywhere the ball gets lost — take a 1 stroke penalty and drop roughly around where you think the ball would have been. Don't worry about re-hitting from where you originally hit.

items: Hole 1: If you don't make it over the water, do NOT re-tee. Go to the other side of the water and hit from there.
If you are in someone's backyard without a fence (hole 4 for example) — move your ball back to the fairway, take a 1 stroke penalty, and hit from there.
Consider being a smart shot taker on the narrower holes near houses. You may not need to hit driver on every par 4.

content.text: Club Length
Play the ball 'up' at a club length — meaning you can move your ball on every shot to find a nice lie. You can move your ball within one club's length of where it was, no closer to the hole.

items: You CAN move from the rough into the fairway if it is within a club's length.
You CANNOT move the ball onto the green.

content.text: Match Play

Play your own ball the entire round. Player A plays against the opposing team's Player A, and Player B plays against their Player B. Keep track of your gross score on each hole and let the commissioner factor in the handicap.
Write down your gross score (what you actually got on the hole), NOT your net score with handicap applied.
Scoring
Each weekly match is worth a total of 6 bonus points, split across three sub-matches:

rows: Lower net score wins. If tied, each team gets 1 point.
Lower net score wins. If tied, each team gets 1 point.
Combined net score of both players. Lower total wins. If tied, each team gets 1 point.

content.text: The winner of each sub-match earns 2 points; the loser gets 0. If a sub-match is tied, each team gets 1 point. A team can earn anywhere from 0 to 6 bonus points per week.
Season standings are determined by cumulative net-vs-par score PLUS total bonus points earned across all weeks. Bonus points reward head-to-head competition on top of raw scoring performance.
Handicaps
Handicaps are calculated as: MIN(20, TRUNC(0.85 × (Average Gross Score − Course Par)))

items: Handicaps update each week based on all rounds played (including pre-season scores).
Negative (plus) handicaps are allowed for players who average below par.
The A player on each team is the one with the lower handicap that week. This can change week to week.

content.text: Net Score
Net Score = Gross Score − Handicap. Net vs Par = Net Score − 33 (course par). A negative net vs par means you played better than par after handicap adjustment.
Gimmes
Gimme putts are at the discretion of your opponent. If they say pick it up, it counts as made. If they don't offer, you putt it out.
Double Par Maximum
For pace of play, pick up your ball after reaching double par on any hole:
Record the double par score for that hole and move on. This keeps the round moving for everyone.
Pace of Play
Be mindful of the groups behind you. If you fall a full hole behind the group ahead, let faster groups play through. Ready golf is encouraged — hit when you're ready, don't wait for honors.
Substitutes

If you can't make a week, find a sub or let the commissioner know. Subs play with your team's partner and their scores/handicaps are tracked separately. Your team still earns bonus points with a sub playing.
